3_11_2: 3.11.2 Scan for vulnerabilities in organizational systems and applications periodically and when new vulnerabilities affecting those systems and applications are identified
3_11_3: 3.11.3 Remediate vulnerabilities in accordance with risk assessments

3_12_4: 3.12.4 Develop, document, and periodically update system security plans that describe system boundaries, system environments of operation, how security requirements are implemented, and the relationships with or connections to other systems

3_13_1: 3.13.1 Monitor, control, and protect communications (i.e., information transmitted or received by organizational systems) at the external boundaries and key internal boundaries of organizational systems
3_13_11: 3.13.11 Employ FIPS-validated cryptography when used to protect the confidentiality of CUI
3_13_15: 3.13.15 Protect the authenticity of communications sessions
3_13_16: 3.13.16 Protect the confidentiality of CUI at rest
3_13_2: 3.13.2 Employ architectural designs, software development techniques, and systems engineering principles that promote effective information security within organizational systems
3_13_3: 3.13.3 Separate user functionality from system management functionality
3_13_4: 3.13.4 Prevent unauthorized and unintended information transfer via shared system resources
3_13_5: 3.13.5 Implement subnetworks for publicly accessible system components that are physically or logically separated from internal networks
3_13_6: 3.13.6 Deny network communications traffic by default and allow network communications traffic by exception (i.e., deny all, permit by exception)
3_13_8: 3.13.8 Implement cryptographic mechanisms to prevent unauthorized disclosure of CUI during transmission unless otherwise protected by alternative physical safeguards
3_14_1: 3.14.1 Identify, report, and correct system flaws in a timely manner
3_14_2: 3.14.2 Provide protection from malicious code at designated locations within organizational systems
3_14_3: 3.14.3 Monitor system security alerts and advisories and take action in response
3_14_4: 3.14.4 Update malicious code protection mechanisms when new releases are available
3_14_6: 3.14.6 Monitor organizational systems, including inbound and outbound communications traffic, to detect attacks and indicators of potential attacks
3_14_7: 3.14.7 Identify unauthorized use of organizational systems
3_1_1: 3.1.1 Limit system access to authorized users, processes acting on behalf of authorized users, and devices (including other systems)
3_1_12: 3.1.12 Monitor and control remote access sessions
3_1_13: 3.1.13 Employ cryptographic mechanisms to protect the confidentiality of remote access sessions
3_1_14: 3.1.14 Route remote access via managed access control points
3_1_2: 3.1.2 Limit system access to the types of transactions and functions that authorized users are permitted to execute
3_1_20: 3.1.20 Verify and control/limit connections to and use of external systems
3_1_3: 3.1.3 Control the flow of CUI in accordance with approved authorizations
3_1_4: 3.1.4 Separate the duties of individuals to reduce the risk of malevolent activity without collusion
3_1_5: 3.1.5 Employ the principle of least privilege, including for specific security functions and privileged accounts
3_1_6: 3.1.6 Use non-privileged accounts or roles when accessing nonsecurity functions
3_1_7: 3.1.7 Prevent non-privileged users from executing privileged functions and capture the execution of such functions in audit logs
3_3_1: 3.3.1 Create and retain system audit logs and records to the extent needed to enable the monitoring, analysis, investigation, and reporting of unlawful or unauthorized system activity
3_3_2: 3.3.2 Ensure that the actions of individual system users can be uniquely traced to those users, so they can be held accountable for their actions
3_3_3: 3.3.3 Review and update logged events
3_3_4: 3.3.4 Alert in the event of an audit logging process failure
3_3_5: 3.3.5 Correlate audit record review, analysis, and reporting processes for investigation and response to indications of unlawful, unauthorized, suspicious, or unusual activity
3_3_8: 3.3.8 Protect audit information and audit logging tools from unauthorized access, modification, and deletion

3_4_1: 3.4.1 Establish and maintain baseline configurations and inventories of organizational systems (including hardware, software, firmware, and documentation) throughout the respective system development life cycles
3_4_2: 3.4.2 Establish and enforce security configuration settings for information technology products employed in organizational systems
3_4_6: 3.4.6 Employ the principle of least functionality by configuring organizational systems to provide only essential capabilities
3_4_7: 3.4.7 Restrict, disable, or prevent the use of nonessential programs, functions, ports, protocols, and services
3_4_9: 3.4.9 Control and monitor user-installed software
3_5_10: 3.5.10 Store and transmit only cryptographically-protected passwords
3_5_2: 3.5.2 Authenticate (or verify) the identities of users, processes, or devices, as a prerequisite to allowing access to organizational systems
3_5_3: 3.5.3 Use multifactor authentication for local and network access to privileged accounts and for network access to non-privileged accounts
3_5_5: 3.5.5 Prevent reuse of identifiers for a defined period
3_5_6: 3.5.6 Disable identifiers after a defined period of inactivity
3_5_7: 3.5.7 Enforce a minimum password complexity and change of characters when new passwords are created
3_5_8: 3.5.8 Prohibit password reuse for a specified number of generations
3_6_1: 3.6.1 Establish an operational incident-handling capability for organizational systems that includes preparation, detection, analysis, containment, recovery, and user response activities
3_6_2: 3.6.2 Track, document, and report incidents to designated officials and/or authorities both internal and external to the organization
